concept: Rich / Full of Quality / Deeply Abundant
description: Having wealth or valuable resources; full of interesting or significant quality. 'Simoe' names the quality of deep value—soil rich in nutrients, experiences rich in meaning, lives rich in connection.
sound_symbolism: The word fills with value. 'Si' begins saturated, quality present. Then 'mo' holds much, value accumulated. Finally 'e' expands outward, richness evident.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'simoe muila' (rich soil). Can describe material wealth but more often describes quality—rich colors, rich flavors, rich experiences.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Rich colors, rich textures, rich ornamentation—Art Nouveau celebrated sensory abundance and detailed beauty.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Rich soil meant everything—the wealth that mattered was fertility, the ability of land to produce life.
